title: Isolation and Culture of Primary Mouse Astrocytes and Neurons
author: Sarah van Veen
materials: Carefully remove enzyme solution from the tissue

steps.title: Coat glass coverslips
steps.instruction: Coat glass coverslips

steps.title: Dissect cortices from P1 pups in ice-cold HBSS-HEPES buffer
steps.instruction: Dissect cortices from P1 pups in ice-cold HBSS-HEPES buffer. - Cut off heads of the pups and transfer them to a 10 cm petri dish with cold HBSS-HEPES - Remove the brain from the skull - Introduce a fine tweezer into the eyes to hold the head - Remove the skin and the skull with a fine curved tweezer. Start at the olfactory bulbus and try the peel off in the direction of the hindbrain. - Scoop the brain using a curved tweezer and transfer it to a small petri dish filled with 1.5 ml HBSS-HEPES and put it on ice. Micro dissection of each brain by using the microscope (binocular) - Hold the hindbrain with tweezers - Use sharp tweezers or small curved scissors to remove both hemispheres from the midbrain/ brainstem - Gently remove meninges from the hemisphere by using tweezers - Isolate the cortex and place in ice cold HBSS-HEPES - Put the cortex in a small petri dish with HBSS-HEPES on ice and cut into smaller pieces and transfer the cortices to a 15ml falcon tube with ice cold HBSS-HEPES.

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C in the water...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C in the water bath for 20 min, mix periodically. Use this time to remove laminin and wash plates, and to prepare the Neurobasal (add the B27 and put in a flask inside the incubator to equilibrate temperature and CO 2 )

steps.title: Wash tissue 3x with 5 ml plating medium
steps.instruction: Wash tissue 3x with 5 ml plating medium

steps.title: Plate 100,000/well onto glass coverslips, swirl...
steps.instruction: Plate 100,000/well onto glass coverslips, swirl the plate gently to spread the cells over the well, incubate the plate for 30 min at RT.

steps.title: Coat 75 cm 2 flasks
steps.instruction: Coat 75 cm 2 flasks

steps.title: Add 10 mL of 20 µg/ml Poly-D-lysine (PDL) per flask
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL of 20 µg/ml Poly-D-lysine (PDL) per flask.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 02:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 02:00:00 . Wash 3x with distilled H 2 O. Add AGM and allow to equilibrate in the incubator.

steps.title: Brain dissection, meninges removal and preparation of single cell suspension
steps.instruction: Brain dissection, meninges removal and preparation of single cell suspension

steps.title: Sacrifice postnatal day 1 (P1) pups...
steps.instruction: Sacrifice postnatal day 1 (P1) pups and wash the heads with sterile PBS

steps.title: Dissect the cortices in ice-cold astrocyte...
steps.instruction: Dissect the cortices in ice-cold astrocyte growth media (AGM), and carefully remove the meninges with fine foreceps

steps.title: Use a flame-polished glass pipette (approximately...
steps.instruction: Use a flame-polished glass pipette (approximately half the original diameter) to homogenize the tissue by pipetting up and down 10-15 times

steps.title: Once the tissue is homogenized, add 9 ml of AGM
steps.instruction: Once the tissue is homogenized, add 9 ml of AGM

steps.title: Further homogenize the tissue by passing...
steps.instruction: Further homogenize the tissue by passing through a 23G needle (10-15 times)

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 g for 10 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 g for 10 min at room temperature

steps.title: Plate at a density of three...
steps.instruction: Plate at a density of three brains per PDL-coated T75 flask

steps.title: Discard the supernatant, add 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant, add 1 ml AGM and repeat step 2.3 and 2.4.

steps.title: Same procedure for shake-off, transfection and...
steps.instruction: Same procedure for shake-off, transfection and co-culture used as in Astrocyte Isolation and Transfection for Astrocytes/Neuron co-culture assay by Sarah van Veen

steps.title: 48h after plating, treat cultures with 5-fluoro-2′-deoxyuridine (FUdR, 5 μM)
steps.instruction: 48h after plating, treat cultures with 5-fluoro-2′-deoxyuridine (FUdR, 5 μM)

steps.title: 48h later, replace medium completely with fresh neuronal culture medium
steps.instruction: 48h later, replace medium completely with fresh neuronal culture medium

steps.title: Remove meninges, olfactory bulb, cerebellum and brainstem
steps.instruction: Remove meninges, olfactory bulb, cerebellum and brainstem.

steps.title: Washing glass cover slips - Wash...
steps.instruction: Washing glass cover slips - Wash coverslips (cs) overnight in Nitric Acid (on wobbler, parafilm covered) - Wash 3x 1 h in Baxter water - Wash 1x in 70% Ethanol - Keep cs in 70 % Ethanol ( parafilm covered) - Place the washed cs in a 24-well plate using tweezers and let dry

steps.title: PDL coating - Use Poly-D Lysine (0
steps.instruction: PDL coating - Use Poly-D Lysine (0.1 mg/ml) - Add 300 µl/well/cs (24-well plate) - Make sure the cs are not floating by pressing them slightly to the bottom with your pipet. - Incubate overnight in the incubator (37°C)

steps.title: Remove PDL coating and let dry...
steps.instruction: Remove PDL coating and let dry the cs - Remove the PDL - Wash 3 times 1 h with Baxter water - Let dry the 24-well plate with cs - Keep at 4°C

steps.title: Laminin coating - Dilute the Laminin...
steps.instruction: Laminin coating - Dilute the Laminin 1/1000 in Baxter water to a final concentration of 1 µg/ml - Add 300 µl/well/cs (24 well plate). - Incubate in the incubator (at least 2h before seeding the primary neurons) - Remove Laminin - Wash once with plating medium (quick wash) - Add 500 µl plating medium - Put in incubator

steps.title: Preheat plating medium at 37°C in...
steps.instruction: Preheat plating medium at 37°C in water bath for at least 20 min.

steps.title: Take a box of ice and...
steps.instruction: Take a box of ice and place HBSS buffer on ice as well as a few small petri dishes with 1.5 ml HBBS-HEPES and one 15 ml tube filled with HBSS-HEPES.

steps.title: Carefully remove the excess of HBSS-HEPES...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the excess of HBSS-HEPES from the falcon tube containing the tissue until we are left with 9 ml, avoiding taking any tissue fragments (tissue will precipitate on the bottom of the tube)

steps.title: Add 1 ml papain-based enzyme solution...
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml papain-based enzyme solution and 50 µl DNase (10 mg/ml)

steps.title: Add 3 ml plating medium and...
steps.instruction: Add 3 ml plating medium and dissociate the tissue with a narrow flamed Pasteur pipette - Gently suck up all tissue, first a little medium, hold the bottom of the pipette close to the bottom of the 15 ml falcon tube (without touching) - Pipette the tissue up and down for 5 to 10 times (don’t blow air bubbles into the medium with the tissue/cells) with ½ flame narrowed pipette, repeat with ¾ flame narrowed pipette. - Centrifuge the dissociated CX single cells solutions @ RT for 5min @1000rpm - Carefully remove the medium from the cell pellet - Add 1 ml of plating medium to the pellet and slowly resuspend the cells by pipetting up and down 3 times. - Add 10 ml of plating medium

steps.title: Place the cells in the incubator...
steps.instruction: Place the cells in the incubator (37°C, 5% CO 2 ) for 2 h

steps.title: After 2 h replace the medium with Neuronal Feeding Media
steps.instruction: After 2 h replace the medium with Neuronal Feeding Media
